label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is asking about the purpose of using so many figures in the text. The options are: (A) record the valuable data (B) provide concrete evidence (C) introduce a research method (D) give a detailed description First, let me analyze the passage. The passage discusses the growth of credit card ownership in China and its implications. It includes several statistics: - 30% of Chinese urban households own at least one credit card - 40% annual growth rate of credit card adoption from 2004 to 2009 - 90% of non-credit card users unaware of safety issues - 60% have little knowledge of credit card debt consequences - 72% of credit card holders never late on payments - 55% able to pay the full balance - 70% say credit cards contributed to overspending These figures are used to support the discussion about the positive and negative effects of credit card use in China, as well as to highlight the need for financial education. Now, let's consider each option: (A) Record the valuable data: This suggests that the figures are just being stored or documented. While the data is valuable, the use of figures in the passage is more than just recording - they are used to support arguments and points being made. (B) Provide concrete evidence: This suggests that the figures are used to support claims and arguments in the passage. For example, the 30% figure supports the claim that credit card ownership is significant, the 90% figure supports the claim that many are unaware of safety issues, etc. This seems to be the case in the passage. (C) Introduce a research method: This would mean the figures are explaining how the research was conducted (e.g., sample size, methodology). However, the passage doesn't really explain how the data was collected or the research methodology. It just presents the results. (D) Give a detailed description: This suggests the figures are being used to paint a detailed picture of the situation. While the figures do help describe the situation, the key point is that they are being used to support the arguments about the implications of credit card use. Given this analysis, option (B) "provide concrete evidence" seems
